assertion.text: Tau spread follows axonal connectivity gradients originating in entorhinal cortex, making structural connectome strength a quantitative predictor of next-stage involvement. Predictions: In longitudinal tau-PET cohorts, regions with higher diffusion-MRI connectivity to currently-involved regions will show tau accumulation at the next imaging timepoint at higher rates than disconnected regions.; Disrupting entorhinal-hippocampal projections in a tau mouse model will reduce hippocampal tau seeding compared to sham controls.

evidence_spans.text: The note claims anatomically patterned spread consistent with Braak's sequence, which begins in transentorhinal regions.

provenance.title: Tau propagation follows predictable Braak staging patterns.md
